warnings.message: Some PDF pages have no strong text-window match in the EPUB
Some dense PDF word windows are missing from the EPUB and may indicate sliced sentence interiors
Some first body lines near the top of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some last body lines near the bottom of PDF pages are not found in the EPUB
Some adjacent EPUB paragraphs look like possible faulty line or page breaks
Some list or roman markers appear embedded in prose instead of starting their own paragraph
Some roman numeral headings appear in body paragraphs instead of centered heading elements
Some chapter headings are long enough to suggest swallowed body text
Some EPUB enumerators look like possible sequence jumps and need triage
Repeated word windows may indicate ghost-layer duplication

What remains is, to inquire, — 1. What benefit or advantage we have by this unction; 2. How this belongs unto our consolation, seeing the Holy Spirit is thus bestowed on us as he is promised to be the comforter of the church.

roman_heading_candidates.text: I. The Holy, Spirit is represented as the EFFICIENT CAUSE, and an inquiry follows: —
I. The evidence of its reality consists in a minute explanation of two passages in Scripture, Zechariah 12:10, and Galatians 4:6, 2, 3.
II. Its general nature is considered, — prayer having been defined to be a spiritual faculty of exercising Christian graces in the way of vocal requests and supplications to God,

overlong_heading_candidates.text: II. This being the substance of what is declared and pleaded for in the preceding treatise, to prevent the obloquy of some and confirm the judgment of others, I shall add the suffrage of ancient and modern writers given unto the principal parts of it, and wher

IV. That there is an especial work of the Holy Spirit, in the supernatural illumination of our minds, needful unto the end proposed, — namely, that we may aright, and according unto our duty, understand the mind of God in the Scripture ourselves, or interpret

V. That hereby alone is that full _assurance of understanding in the_ _knowledge of the mystery of God,_ his truth and grace, to be obtained, whereby any man may answer the mind and will of God, or comply with his own duty in all that he may be called to do or

VI. The certainty and assurance that we may have and ought to have of our right understanding the mind of God in the Scripture, either in general or as to any especial doctrine, doth not depend upon, is not resolved into, any immediate inspiration or enthusias

VII. That whereas the means of the right interpretation of the Scripture, and understanding of the mind of God therein, are of two sorts, — first, such as are prescribed unto us in a way of duty, as _prayer, meditation_ on the word itself, and the like; and, s

XI. The discourse on Spiritual Gifts, though comparatively short, is the second part of the main.body of the whole work on the Spirit; and, from various allusions to it in other works of the author, he seems to trove attached considerable importance to it.
The Holy Ghost the comforter of the church by way of office — How he is the church's advocate — John 14:16; 1 John 2:1, 2; John 16:8-11 opened.

I. In our inquiry after the first, or what are the gifts whereby men are fitted and enabled for the ministry, we wholly set aside the consideration of all those gracious qualifications of faith, love, zeal, compassion, careful tender watchfulness, and the like

orphan_candidates.context: (2.) Moreover, the effect of this work of the Holy Spirit on the minds of men doth evidence of what nature it is, And this, also, is variously expressed; as, —
